repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Fit metal pipes for a permanent fix
Replacement often £4,500–£7,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC? Check These First
buyIf: * Supercharger coupler recently replaced — no rattle means a healthy unit
* Coolant crossover pipes replaced with metal aftermarket units
* Documented timing chain tensioner history (pre-2010 examples)
* Full service history with 7,500–10,000 mile oil changes
avoidIf: * Any supercharger rattle at idle — coupler wear is almost certain
* Evidence of coolant loss or overheating (crossover pipe failure risk)
* Poor service history — the supercharged V8 is unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 4.2 SC is a genuine performance icon — 390bhp of supercharged V8 in a Range Rover Sport body. It's a thrilling, characterful engine that's fundamentally robust, but it demands respect for its cooling system and supercharger wear items. A well-maintained 4.2 SC with metal coolant pipes and a fresh supercharger coupler is a wonderful thing to own. Buy on history, maintain the supercharger, and enjoy the noise.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup; supercharger rattle at idle is the key warning
* Supercharger Health — Any rattling from the supercharger, especially when warm? Coupler wear
* Service History — 7,500–10,000 mile oil changes are essential for the supercharged V8
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss; inspect around crossover pipes for leaks
* Boost Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation or loss of power?
* Timing Chain History — Pre-2010 tensioners need checking; any replacement done?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (catalyst/sensor issues)

items.question: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC engine costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,400–£2,200. These are specialist-only replacements — the supercharged AJ-V8 requires Land Rover/Jaguar specialist expertise. A supercharger coupler replacement costs £400–£800.

items.question: Is the Land Rover Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC reliable?
items.answer: The 4.2 SC is a robust and characterful engine, but it has known weak points. The supercharger coupler wears (rattle at idle, £400–£800), the plastic coolant crossover pipes fail (coolant loss, £800–£2,000), and pre-2010 timing chain tensioners need monitoring (£1,500–£3,500). Regular maintenance and proactive replacement of the known weak points make it a reliable performance engine.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC?
items.answer: Supercharger coupler wear (rattling at idle, £400–£800), plastic coolant crossover pipe failure (coolant loss, overheating, £800–£2,000), and timing chain tensioner wear on pre-2010 examples (£1,500–£3,500). The supercharger is generally durable if the coupler is maintained.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Range Rover Sport L320 values range from £4,000–£15,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £4,500–£7,500 reconditioned engine represents 30–60% of vehicle value — our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues, assess scrap value first.

items.question: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover Sport 4.2 SC engine code?
items.answer: The Range Rover Sport L320 4.2 SC uses the AJ-V8 4.2 SC engine code — a supercharged 4.2-litre V8 producing 390bhp. This is the same engine used in the Jaguar XKR, XFR and other performance models. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the supercharger coupler issue, or does the supercharger need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — in many cases the coupler alone can be replaced (£400–£800) without needing a full supercharger rebuild (£1,500–£4,000). The coupler is a spring-loaded wear item; replacement restores smooth, quiet operation. At the first sign of rattle at idle, book an inspection — ignored coupler wear can damage the supercharger's internal bearings.
